title: senecio volckmannii var. volckmannii
intro: Senecio volckmannii var. volckmannii, also known as Volckmann's ragwort, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to the Andes mountains. The plant contains a compound called juncusin, which has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. In terms of health benefits, Volckmann's ragwort has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, although its effectiveness has not been extensively scientifically proven. Herbal preparations of Senecio volckmannii var. volckmannii can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, but its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and side effects associated with its pyrrolizidine alkaloid content.
